title: xShellz offers persistent sandboxed Linux boxes for coding agents
friendlyTitle: Agent Shell gives coding agents their own protected Linux computer
summary: xShellz has introduced Agent Shell, a persistent Ubuntu environment that coding agents can reach over SSH or a browser and control as root. The service uses gVisor isolation, resource caps and live monitoring, with Claude Code, Codex, Gemini and its own agent preinstalled.
friendlySummary: Agent Shell is a hosted Ubuntu box where AI coding tools can install software and run services. It is separated from the host system and includes live resource monitoring.
whyItMatters: A disposable or isolated machine can reduce the damage caused by a coding agent with broad permissions. Users should still review egress, secrets, persistence, pricing and provider security before moving sensitive repositories into any hosted sandbox.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Giving an agent a separate computer is safer than giving it full control of your main machine. Do not place secrets or private code there until you have checked the service’s security rules.
